rationales.achievement: She signs Arjun up for Olympiad prep because she reads her children's report cards like her own "performance reviews" and wants him to "rise to the challenge."
rationales.power: She enrolls him without telling Ramesh or Arjun and keeps "adding" work because her son is "obedient," showing control over others' choices.
rationales.conformity: Her thinking is heavily shaped by comparison and social pressure from "family gathering" talk and Priya mentioning "her Rohan" at the temple.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes she may be doing this for status rather than him, and that Arjun "just absorbs it" while she keeps pushing more onto him.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on pongal rice made "the way she's done it for sixty years" and her desire to learn it by standing beside Amma until "my hands remember."
rationales.benevolence: She is moved that Amma cooked despite bad knees because Arjun "missed her pongal rice," and wants to preserve that care by learning how to make it properly.

rationales.conformity: She frames the act simply as "Amma asked, I did it," emphasizing compliance with family expectations rather than personal preference.
rationales.tradition: Reorganizing the prayer room because the Lakshmi lamp was "in the wrong position" reflects maintaining a religious household custom.

rationales.conformity: She says "no before I even thought about it," enforcing the expected family line instead of allowing negotiation.
rationales.tradition: Refusing to let Divya skip the "temple youth group" for a birthday party prioritizes a religious family obligation over a peer event.
rationales.benevolence: She sees her daughter has "given up trying to negotiate" and still recognizes she is "running the same script," which undermines responsiveness to her child's needs.

rationales.achievement: She came to the Pongal committee meeting with "the spreadsheet ready this time," showing preparation and competence in managing the event.
rationales.tradition: Her work is directed toward organizing a "Pongal committee meeting," supporting an established cultural celebration.

rationales.achievement: She treats Arjun's low result as "Baseline" and insists "you start somewhere," keeping focus on improvement and performance progress.

rationales.security: The entry revolves around Amma's fall, whether the family needs "a helper," and worry about whether the current arrangement is still sustainable.
rationales.tradition: She hesitates because Amma's routines around "morning puja," spice boxes, and household order feel like something important that would be betrayed.
rationales.benevolence: She is stretched across Sanjay's tournament and Amma's injury, and her conflict comes from not wanting to hurt Amma while trying to care for the family practically.

rationales.achievement: She highlights that Divya had been "grinding at it for months" and played the recital piece "clean, start to finish," emphasizing disciplined accomplishment.
rationales.benevolence: Her reaction is deeply caring: she feels her "chest crack open a little" and searches for a way to tell Divya something affirming without turning it into another evaluation.

rationales.achievement: The entry centers on Arjun's score rising to "sixty-first percentile" and on interpreting it as progress toward a larger target.
rationales.benevolence: She realizes she answered with "good, keep going" instead of appreciating his happiness in the moment, repeating the gap-focused pattern she disliked in her father.

rationales.self_direction: She is drawn to "joining her school project in Kenya" and admits the honest answer to staying put is "no," prioritizing her own chosen path over settling.
rationales.stimulation: She feels the current life is "slowly turning to stone" while her heart raced at the idea of building a project in Kenya from scratch.
rationales.security: The speeches about "building foundations" and "putting down roots" feel suffocating to her, showing active discomfort with stability and permanence.
rationales.universalism: What excites her is teaching "environmental science to kids who've never seen a glacier," reflecting concern for education and a broader world beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She independently "booked the flight to Nairobi" and told the school she is taking a sabbatical, choosing her own direction despite others' likely reactions.
rationales.stimulation: Booking a preliminary visit to Nairobi shows active pursuit of a new, uncertain experience rather than remaining in her existing routine.
rationales.security: She pushes aside the practical problem of the farmhouse as "just logistics" and leaves the stable setup unresolved while she goes.

rationales.achievement: She says that if she is going to put "my name on something, it should be done properly," showing pride in doing the work well.
rationales.conformity: She carefully tailors each report card comment so no parent feels "embarrassed or caught off guard," reflecting restraint to meet social expectations.
rationales.benevolence: She spends extra effort choosing the right wording because "different families need different approaches," showing attentive care toward students and parents.

rationales.self_direction: She suppresses her own judgment and "nodded too," ending with "why should I be the one" instead of speaking independently.
rationales.security: She explicitly prefers that "students need structure and clear expectations" and views project-based learning as creating "chaos in practice."
rationales.conformity: She goes along because "new vice-principals need to feel heard" and nobody else objects, prioritizing smooth relations over voicing disagreement.

rationales.security: She does nothing partly to avoid complaints from "Darren's parents," involvement from the vice-principal, and the fallout of a public confrontation.
rationales.conformity: Her main hesitation is that speaking up would have made "a scene" and caused others to feel judged, so she preserves social smoothness by staying silent.
rationales.benevolence: She admits "I should have been the one to defend him" but kept walking, leaving Mr. Goh unsupported after a disrespectful joke.

rationales.security: She describes the weekly call with her mother as "comfortable. Or at least it's familiar," showing attachment to predictability and routine.
rationales.conformity: Both sides stay within the same indirect script where "we both pretend everything that needs to be said has been said," preserving harmony by not challenging expectations.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes the seriousness of the loss through "two months of planning," "three revised proposals," and confidence that the model is "good work."
rationales.power: He is incensed that others are now in the conversation after the brief came to him through prior prestige, and says what matters is that people "honor their word" to him.

rationales.achievement: He leaves the meeting "feeling solid about the project" and immediately thinks through the substantial fee and next steps for securing it.
rationales.power: He reads the family hierarchy quickly and plans to tailor the next presentation because "taste steers the ship," showing strategic attention to influence and control.
rationales.security: He prefers private commissions because they are "cleaner" with "one family, one vision, one contract," valuing predictability over messy public processes.

rationales.hedonism: After an unpleasant family dinner, he returns home and opens "a 2015 Barolo I'd been saving," choosing immediate sensory comfort.

rationales.achievement: He argues other artists "haven't done a single public piece over ten meters," framing the commission as something he merited through superior record and experience.
rationales.power: He says the opportunity should have been "mine by right" because he had spent years building obligations and expected those relationships to produce returns.

rationales.self_direction: He envies Joachim's ability to "just make things" and is troubled by how far he has drifted from work that comes from somewhere inside himself.
rationales.power: He is weary that "every conversation I have lately is a negotiation" about credit, control, and status, signaling disillusionment with that orientation.

rationales.hedonism: He relishes the evening's pleasures, from wearing "my grey Zegna and the Patek" to driving home "feeling satisfied."
rationales.achievement: He frames the recognition as something he has "earned ... through decades of difficult work," treating the event as confirmation of accomplishment.
rationales.power: He explicitly values "the standing they create" and the ability to enter a room and have people know who he is and what he has accomplished.

rationales.self_direction: "No audience. Nothing to prove to anyone" and focusing only on "me and the clay" show a return to self-authored work rather than external performance.
rationales.hedonism: Even with a sore back, he says he "felt better than I have in weeks" and caps the day by opening "a good Riesling" to enjoy what he made.
rationales.power: He contrasts the day with the usual "performance involved" and values creating without managing others' perceptions or status.

rationales.self_direction: He tells the Beckers "the subtle piece would be better art" even though it means less money, choosing the work he genuinely wants to make.
rationales.hedonism: He says "I'll actually enjoy making this one" and that having more days alone with clay matters more than he expected.
rationales.achievement: He favors the smaller maquette because it is simply "better art," prioritizing excellence in the work itself over spectacle.
rationales.power: He explicitly rejects the larger status play, saying "the status game has diminishing returns" and refusing to steer them toward the more grandiose, expensive option.
